Legacy Projects

The OWLS Project — More than 50 large, cosmological, hydrodynamical simulations aim to study a variety of problems in astrophysical cosmology.

The GIMIC Project — A campaign of large-scale hydrodynamical simulations of the Lambda-Cold Dark Matter Universe.

The Aquarius Project — A set of six examples of an isolated halo similar in mass to that of the Milky Way.

The Millennium Project — The largest N-Body Simulation ever carried out back in 2005, containing over 10 billion particles.

The Hubble Volume Project — A joint effort to study the formation of clusters of galaxies, filaments and void-structures.

The GIF Project — A study of the formation and evolution of galaxies using semi-analytical galaxy formation models embedded in large high-resolution N-body simulations.
